properties.remark: Corrects previous hail report from 2 S Appalachia.

*** 2 FATAL, 2 INJ *** Tornado may have started in southeast Fentress County (pending OHX survey) before tracking into forested areas of Morgan County that were inaccessible to the survey team. The tornado moved southeast where it produced EF-2 damage, destroyed two mobile homes, and damaged multiple barns and outbuildings near the intersection of Twin Bridge Rd and Thurman Lavender Rd. As the tornado continued southeast, it snapped the top of trees consistent with EF-1 damage near the convergence of Highway 62 and Deer Lodge Highway. The tornado continued southeast into forested areas inaccessible to the survey team before dissipating prior to Highway 27.
Several homes damaged and trees down. Reports of fatalities but number unknown. Possible tornado.
Several homes damaged and trees down. Possible tornado.
Several trees down.
A few trees down.
Several trees down.
Several trees down as well.

The tornado began near McAnally Rd and Campground Rd before moving east along Highway 131. There were several barns and outbuildings with roof damage, and numerous snapped trees. The most significant damage was to a large barn on Highway 131 that was completely destroyed. Damage continued to around Clinch River Hardwoods and Thorn Hill Church. A large tanker trailer was overturned, and the church had some light damage to siding and windows. The path ended where trees were snapped on the side of Clinch Mountain near Highway 25E.

Delayed Report: Several large trees scattered about were felled by severe thunderstorm winds on private property and seen in southwestern Meigs County, on both sides of the Hiwassee River and near Highway 58 for a few miles north of the river. One of the trees on the private land was snapped, the rest were felled at their roots. At least one road was impassable due to the tree damage. Time estimated by radar.

*** 5 INJ *** EF0 with 85 mph max winds. Path length 2 miles. Path width 200 yards. Most damage indicators along Holt Road and New Hope Rd. Injuries occurred with camper trailers that were rolled by the winds.

A Facebook report of storm damage with a couple videos showcasing the following damage: Couple dozen medium to large trees were blown down along Kell Loop Road outside of Dunlap, TN. A small trailer was blown onto its side, off of the hitch. Another small trailer was dragged in the mud a couple feet. Two porch chairs were blown and dropped on a fence. A couple of chickens were killed. Several trees fell on power lines, resulting in a few power outages. One mailbox was separated from its post and blown into a ditch, underneath the powerlines. Time estimated by radar.

Standing water visible in front yards. 2.5 inches of rain received at time of second report.

Minor flooding report from spotter as follows: Near the intersection of Opie Arnold Rd (TN SR 75) and Jaynes Rd. Small stream runs through a tile that goes under SR 75 . Normal water depth is around 3 inches , and there is approximately 13 inches between stream bed and the road . Stream is completely full with approximately 3 inches of water running over SR 75.

Social media image of water flowing over a driveway and through a hay field, a couple inches deep eyeball estimate. Per the commenter: The waters are overflowing Pisgah Road and our driveway. The waters have receded just a little at time of report. Highest water along the road in the four years the persons been in the location.
